title: CLI: Geo-Node Statistics Command
description: Add a blender-cli stats command that outputs quantitative metrics for a node group applied to a test mesh. During quilting development, we repeatedly wrote ad-hoc exec scripts to measure vertex counts, displacement ranges, stitch counts, bounding box extents, and Z-value distributions. A dedicated stats command would output these metrics in structured JSON. Metrics to include: vertex/edge/face/polygon counts, bounding box min/max/extent, displacement range (min/max Z or normal offset), unique value counts for sampled attributes, and derived metrics like stitch_count = (total_verts - base_verts) / verts_per_stitch.
definition-of-done: 1. blender-cli stats --group Quilting outputs JSON with vertex_count, edge_count, face_count, bbox_min, bbox_max, bbox_extent, z_range (or displacement_range), and optionally stitch_count. 2. Accepts --mesh and --params flags (same as BLEN-22). 3. Output is machine-parseable JSON for use in scripts and CI.
testing-plan: 1. stats --group Quilting — verify counts match manual exec measurements. 2. stats --group Quilting --params '{"Socket_11": true}' — verify stitch count is reported. 3. Compare stats output across scale changes to confirm scaling behavior.
todos: Compute mesh metrics (counts, bbox, z-range) from evaluated mesh
unknowns: Should stats include timing information (how long the node group takes to evaluate)? Useful for performance regression testing but adds complexity.
